fr.recommendations: Improving emergency responder access could be achieved by ensuring clear and unobstructed access routes to all structures, particularly given the limited side access and presence of multiple structures. Maintaining clear drive aisles and minimizing dense vegetation along these pathways may enhance responder movement and visibility. Strategically managing vegetation in the landscaped area and open green space could further support efficient operations by preventing any impediments to responder navigation. Designating clear signage for each structure with visible route indicators at decision points along the access routes could facilitate quicker navigation and effective staging during emergency operations. This approach may assist emergency responders in efficiently maneuvering around the property, ensuring timely and effective response efforts.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space and reducing fuel continuity around the property could be achieved by increasing the separation between vegetation and structures, particularly where vegetation is adjacent to the building. Thinning out dense tree canopies and breaking up tree clusters may reduce the likelihood of fire spread through crown fuels, while managing vegetation along the access route could support firefighter access and egress. Modifying the vegetated perimeter and maintaining clear side and access routes can improve access for fire suppression and emergency response efforts. Prioritizing vegetation management around the structure and along the access route would promote long-term wildfire resilience, while creating open space buffers around the residence could further reduce potential exposure to wildfire.
